questions.question: What is motion?
questions.answer: Motion is the change in position of an object with respect to time.

questions.question: What is a reference point in motion?
questions.answer: A reference point is a fixed point used to describe the position of an object.

questions.question: What is distance?
questions.answer: Distance is the total path length travelled by an object.

questions.question: What is displacement?
questions.answer: Displacement is the shortest distance between the initial and final positions of an object.

questions.question: Can displacement be zero even when distance is not zero?
questions.answer: Yes, displacement can be zero if the object returns to its starting point.

questions.question: What is uniform motion?
questions.answer: Uniform motion is when an object travels equal distances in equal intervals of time.

questions.question: What is non-uniform motion?
questions.answer: Non-uniform motion occurs when an object travels unequal distances in equal intervals of time.

questions.question: What is speed?
questions.answer: Speed is the distance travelled by an object per unit time.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of speed?
questions.answer: The SI unit of speed is metre per second (m/s).

questions.question: What is average speed?
questions.answer: Average speed is the total distance travelled divided by total time taken.

questions.question: What is velocity?
questions.answer: Velocity is the speed of an object in a given direction.

questions.question: Is velocity a scalar or vector quantity?
questions.answer: Velocity is a vector quantity.

questions.question: What is acceleration?
questions.answer: Acceleration is the rate of change of velocity with time.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of acceleration?
questions.answer: The SI unit of acceleration is metre per second squared (m/s²).

questions.question: What is uniform acceleration?
questions.answer: Uniform acceleration occurs when velocity changes by equal amounts in equal intervals of time.

questions.question: What does a distance-time graph represent?
questions.answer: A distance-time graph shows how distance changes with time.

questions.question: What does the slope of a distance-time graph represent?
questions.answer: The slope represents the speed of the object.

questions.question: What does a velocity-time graph represent?
questions.answer: A velocity-time graph shows how velocity changes with time.

questions.question: What does the area under a velocity-time graph represent?
questions.answer: The area under the graph represents displacement.

questions.question: Write the first equation of motion.
questions.answer: v = u + at

questions.question: Write the second equation of motion.
questions.answer: s = ut + 1/2 at²

questions.question: Write the third equation of motion.
questions.answer: v² − u² = 2as

questions.question: What is uniform circular motion?
questions.answer: Uniform circular motion is motion along a circular path with constant speed.

questions.question: Give one example of circular motion.
questions.answer: The motion of the Earth around the Sun.

questions.question: Does velocity change in uniform circular motion?
questions.answer: Yes, velocity changes because the direction of motion continuously changes.
